channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: African Nations Seek Greater Control of Mineral Wealth for Sustainable Growth
item.description: African nations are increasingly embracing a wave of resource nationalism, aiming to ensure that the continent benefits more equitably from its abundant mineral resources. This movement is gaining tra...

item.title: Southern African Countries Unite to Combat Illicit Trade in Strategic Minerals
item.description: Southern African countries under the Southern African Development Community (SADC) are intensifying efforts to protect the region’s valuable mineral resources from organised crime and illegal trade. T...

item.title: Pakistan Facilitates Indirect US-Iran Talks to Reduce Conflict
item.description: Islamabad has taken a central role in trying to reduce tensions between the United States and Iran, with support from Turkey and Egypt. While immediate face-to-face negotiations remain unlikely, indir...

item.title: Tanzania’s Taifa Stars suffer narrow 1-0 defeat to Liechtenstein in Fifa Series opener
item.description: Tanzania’s national football team, Taifa Stars, began their Fifa Series campaign on a disappointing note after losing 1–0 to Liechtenstein at Pele Stadium in Kigali on March 26, 2026. The match was ti...

item.title: Conflict Escalation Expected After US Pause Ends on Day 27 of US-Israeli War on Iran
item.description: On the 27th day of the US-Israeli conflict with Iran, hostilities have intensified with no sign of de-escalation following the expiry of former US President Donald Trump’s five-day pause. The conflict...

item.title: US Court Reviews Maduro’s Access to Venezuelan Funds for Legal Defence Amid Sanctions
item.description: A US federal judge in Manhattan is questioning whether barring Venezuelan ex-president Nicolas Maduro from using his country's public funds for his legal defence violates his constitutional rights in ...

item.title: Conflict intensifies as US-Israel and Iran brace for wider confrontation
item.description: The 27th day of the US-Israeli conflict against Iran saw no signs of de-escalation as a five-day US-announced pause came to an end, suggesting that a broader and more dangerous phase could soon unfold...

item.title: AfCFTA Seen as Key to Africa’s Economic Transformation
item.description: African leaders and economists are increasingly looking at the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) as a vital step towards reshaping the continent’s economy and boosting self-reliance. With g...

item.title: Conflict Intensifies Across Iran, Lebanon and Regional Fronts After US Pause Ends
item.description: On the 27th day of the US-Israeli war on Iran, tensions have sharply escalated as President Trump's five-day pause has ended. Over the past 24 hours, Iran faced multiple direct strikes in Tehran and o...

item.title: US-Israel Conflict with Iran Poised for Intensification across Multiple Fronts
item.description: On the 27th day of the US-Israeli war on Iran, the situation has turned increasingly volatile as President Donald Trump’s temporary five-day pause expires. Both sides now seem to be gearing up for a m...

item.title: Hertfordshire police resume investigation into Andrew Tate over historic sexual abuse allegations
item.description: Hertfordshire Constabulary has announced it will reopen an investigation into Andrew Tate concerning allegations of rape and sexual assault dating back to 2013-2015. The initial investigation, launche...

item.title: PM Shehbaz and MQM-P leaders discuss political situation and development projects in Sindh
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif met a delegation from the Muttahida Qaumi Movement-Pakistan (MQM-P) in Karachi, where he appreciated the efforts of former Sindh governor Kamran Tessori, recently replace...

item.title: NASA Adjusts Lunar Plans: Base Details Emerge as Gateway Station is Scrapped
item.description: Recent reports detail emerging plans for a NASA lunar base, coinciding with the cancellation of the planned Lunar Gateway station. While the Gateway station, intended to orbit the Moon as a staging po...

item.title: Noida International Airport poised to strengthen Uttar Pradesh’s economy with increased connectivity and investment
item.description: The Uttar Pradesh government announced that the Noida International Airport at Jewar, set to be inaugurated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on 28 March 2026, will play a pivotal role in transforming t...

item.title: Sri Ram Navami procession in Belagavi features vibrant celebrations
item.description: The Sri Ram Navami celebrations in Belagavi on March 26, 2026, were marked by a lively and colourful procession through the city’s main streets. The event, which was inaugurated by Deputy Commissioner...

item.title: Fourth Pakistani killed in UAE after missile debris falls in Abu Dhabi
item.description: In a tragic development, the fourth Pakistani citizen has lost his life in the United Arab Emirates following the recent missile escalation. The fatal incident took place on Sweihan Street, a mixed re...

item.title: IOC introduces new policy requiring gene test for eligibility in female Olympic events
item.description: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) has announced a new global policy that restricts participation in female category events at the Olympic Games to biological women only. Under the new rules, a...

item.title: Nepal panel recommends prosecution of ex‑PM KP Sharma Oli over deadly 2025 youth uprising
item.description: A Nepal government‑backed commission has recommended that former prime minister KP Sharma Oli be investigated and prosecuted over the deadly September 2025 youth uprising that toppled his government, ...

item.title: Pakistan Resumes Operation Ghazab lil-Haq Against Afghan Taliban After Eid Pause
item.description: The Foreign Office (FO) of Pakistan has announced that the temporary pause in Operation Ghazab lil-Haq has officially ended and military actions will continue until all objectives are met. The operati...

item.title: Invasive Indian crows threaten Tanzania’s native bird species and ecotourism
item.description: In Tanzania, the growing population of invasive house crows, locally known as 'Kunguru wa Zanzibar' and scientifically called Corvus splendens, is posing a serious threat to the country’s native bird ...
